what_happened: deploy-production.yml had contents: read which blocks gh release create
why: The gh CLI uses GITHUB_TOKEN (or GH_TOKEN set from it) for release creation, which requires contents: write permission at the job level. The original comment incorrectly stated write was not needed.
fix_applied: Changed job-level permissions from contents: read to contents: write and updated comment to explain why.
pattern_to_remember: When adding gh release create to a GitHub Actions workflow, the job must have contents: write permission. The gh CLI's GH_TOKEN env var for release creation maps to GITHUB_TOKEN permissions, not app token permissions.

what_happened: gh release create accepts --notes-file to read release body from a file
why: The changelog was already written to $CHANGELOG_PATH (changelog.md) by an earlier step, so --notes-file avoids shell quoting issues with multi-line content.
pattern_to_remember: For gh release create with multi-line bodies, use --notes-file <path> rather than --notes "$(cat file)" to avoid quoting/newline issues.
